aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: maritime land force service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Marine Corps (USMC), also referred to as the United States Marines or simply the Marines, is the naval infantry service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: Indiana National Guard training base

surface_forms: Camp Joseph T. Robinson in Arkansas
description: military camp in North Little Rock, Arkansas

aliases: Camp Meade Cantonment and Field Signal School
description: United States Army installation
U.S. military facility in New Jersey
description_wiki: Camp Kilmer is a former United States Army camp in Central New Jersey that was activated in June 1942 as a staging area and part of an installation of the New York Port of Embarkation.

description: Largest U.S. Army embarkation camp used during World War II
description_wiki: Camp Shanks was a United States Army installation in the Orangeburg, New York area.

description: Cunard Line ocean liner, also used as a merchant cruiser and troop transport
description_wiki: RMS Aquitania was a British ocean liner operated by the Cunard Line from 1914 to 1950.

description: northern area of the Atlantic ocean
country in north-west Europe, part of the United Kingdom
description_wiki: Scotland is a country that is part of the United Kingdom.

description: capital and largest city of England and the United Kingdom
description_wiki: London is the capital and largest city of England and the United Kingdom, with a population of 9.1 million people in 2024.

description: country in Western Europe and other continents (through its overseas territories in America, Africa and Oceania)
description_wiki: France, officially the French Republic, is a country primarily located in Western Europe.

description: commune in Seine-Maritime, France
description_wiki: Le Havre is a major port city in the Seine-Maritime department in the Normandy region of northern France.

description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

surface_forms: Winter of '44, of course that was the Bulge
description: 1944-1945 World War II battle
description_wiki: The Battle of the Bulge, also known as the Ardennes Offensive, was the last major German offensive campaign on the Western Front during the Second World War, taking place from 16 December 1944 to 25 January 1945.

description: country in Western Europe
description_wiki: Luxembourg,  officially the Grand Duchy of Luxembourg, is a landlocked country in Western Europe.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

description: German defensive line used in World War II
description_wiki: The Siegfried Line, known in German as the Westwall ([ˈvɛstˌval], 'western rampart'), was a German defensive line built during the late 1930s.

description: dictator of Germany from 1933 to 1945, main instigator of World War II (1889–1945)
description_wiki: Adolf Hitler (20 April 1889 – 30 April 1945) was an Austrian-born  German politician who was the dictator of Germany during the Nazi era from 1933 until his suicide in 1945.

description: country in western Europe
description_wiki: Belgium, officially the Kingdom of Belgium, is a country in Northwestern Europe.
